message:
fix(markdown-import): allow titles with colons + cross-reference parents in same import

Two related improvements to `br create -f issues.md` that come up
when an import file describes several issues that reference each
other by title or by short standin id.

src/util/markdown_import.rs::parse_dependency
  Previously, any `foo:bar` token in a `[depends_on: ...]` line was
  parsed as a `(type=foo, id=bar)` dep edge, and only validated
  *after* the split. If `foo` wasn't one of the known dependency
  types we returned `(foo, bar, valid=false)`, which the importer
  surfaced as `warning: skipping invalid dependency type` and the
  edge was silently dropped.
  That broke the natural-feeling input
  `[depends_on: Task: With colon]`, where "Task" is the user's title
  prefix, not a dep-type tag. We now check the type first: if
  `validate_dependency_type(type_part).is_some()` the existing
  `(type, id, true)` tuple wins, otherwise we fall through and
  treat the whole `dep_str` as the id with default type `blocks` —
  i.e. the importer now tries to resolve `"Task: With colon"` as an
  id/title in the same way as a no-colon dep.
  In-file unit test updates the previously-asserted
  `("invalid", "bd-789", false)` shape to the new
  `("blocks", "invalid:bd-789", true)` shape.
  tests/markdown_import.rs::* swaps the matching `expected warning
  for invalid dependency type` assertion to `Issue not found:
  invalid-type:bd-123`, which is what the importer now emits when
  the resolver fails to find that title (no longer a parse-time
  rejection).

src/cli/commands/create.rs::execute_import
  When importing a multi-issue markdown file, the parent field for
  each issue was being resolved via `resolve_issue_id(storage, ...)`
  *only*, which means parents could only be by-id references to
  already-stored issues. If you wrote
      ## My Epic
      ### ID
      epic1
      ## My Task
      ### Parent
      epic1
  the parent of `My Task` failed to resolve because `epic1` is the
  in-file standin id, not yet a storage id. We now consult the
  `standin_to_id` and `title_to_id` maps that the importer is
  already building for `depends_on`, falling through to the storage
  resolver only when neither hit. Same lookup rules as the existing
  dep resolver, just applied to the parent field.

New regression coverage:
  tests/test_create_deps_colon.rs — creates "Task: With colon",
    then drives a markdown import with
    `[depends_on: Task: With colon]` and asserts the dep landed.
  tests/test_create_parent_standin.rs — drives the epic1/My Task
    pair above through `br create -f` and asserts the import
    succeeds (i.e. the parent resolves via the in-file standin).
